title: Claude Code Swarm Orchestration Skill
summary: TeammateTool enables persistent multi-agent teams with inbox messaging Three spawn backends (in-process, tmux, iterm2) serve different visibility needs
creator_view: TeammateTool enables persistent multi-agent teams with inbox messaging Three spawn backends (in-process, tmux, iterm2) serve different visibility needs

candidates.name: 6-pattern orchestration taxonomy for AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md
candidates.description:
Parallel Specialists, Pipeline, Swarm, Research+Implementation, Plan Approval, Coordinated Refactoring. Compare against existing patterns in AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md and identify which patterns your skills use implicitly but haven't named.

Extraction action: Map each pattern to existing skill usage: deep-research=Research+Impl, skill-audit=Plan Approval, code-reviewer=Parallel Specialists. Identify unnamed patterns.

candidates.name: Spawn backend comparison for T5 worktree management
candidates.description:
in-process (hidden, fastest), tmux (visible, persistent), iterm2 (macOS split). Auto-detection logic. Directly relevant to T5 worktree management and parallel CC instance strategies.

Extraction action: Update T5 worktree management todo with backend comparison. Test tmux backend for visible parallel agent execution.

candidates.name: TeammateTool 13-operation reference for agent team definitions
candidates.description:
Full operation lifecycle for persistent teams. Compare against your .claude/teams/ definitions — are you using all available operations? Are your teams missing shutdown/cleanup protocols?

Extraction action: Audit .claude/teams/ against the 13 operations. Check if audit-review-team and research-plan-team use requestShutdown/cleanup.

candidates.name: Task dependency auto-unblock for skill pipeline design
candidates.description:
Declarative task dependencies via blockedBy arrays. Auto-unblock on completion. Could replace manual orchestration in multi-phase skills like deep-research and repo-synthesis.

Extraction action: Evaluate whether deep-research phases (searcher -> synthesizer -> verifier) could use TaskCreate with blockedBy instead of manual sequential dispatch.

candidates.name: Subagent vs teammate decision framework
candidates.description:
Short-lived focused work = subagent (Task tool alone). Persistent coordination with messaging = teammate (Task + team). Clear decision criteria for when to use which.

Extraction action: Add to AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md as a decision framework. Currently your skills use subagents exclusively — evaluate whether any workflows benefit from persistent teammates.

site.title: Claude Code Swarm Orchestration Skill
site.description: Complete guide to multi-agent coordination with TeammateTool, Task system, and orchestration patterns
site.author: kieranklaassen

absence_patterns: No convergence or quality verification of agent outputs
No compaction handling or state persistence for long-running teams
No discussion of agent stalling or context exhaustion
No cost or token budget considerations
No treatment of cross-session team persistence
key_claims: TeammateTool enables persistent multi-agent teams with inbox messaging
Three spawn backends (in-process, tmux, iterm2) serve different visibility needs
Task dependency auto-unblocking creates declarative pipelines
Subagents and teammates serve different coordination patterns
Write is preferable to broadcast for team communication
Six patterns cover most multi-agent coordination needs
